summary: Kenneth demonstrates his $180/week HIMS covered call strategy during a volatile earnings week. He discusses position sizing discipline, avoiding high-risk earnings plays despite premium temptation, and executing trades across HIMS, QuantumCape, and Enphase with varying premium targets while managing portfolio risk.
takeaways: Avoid selling covered calls on stocks with huge recent run-ups before earnings—premium is high but downside risk is severe
Position sizing: ease into unfamiliar stocks gradually (Kenneth bought 500 HIMS shares but only 500 QuantumCape shares pre-market at $11.47 due to unfamiliarity)
Accept lower premiums on defensive positions—Enphase trade netted only 2.7% but Kenneth prioritizes premium capture over upside participation
Target $180 premium per 100 shares per week on HIMS; Kenneth achieved $206 on 100-share equivalent (2 contracts × $416 ÷ 2)
Strike selection: sell calls 1-2 strikes above current price ($58 calls on $57.55 HIMS, $115 calls on $114.70 QuantumCape) for high probability assignment
Sit out high-risk weeks—Kenneth explicitly avoided earnings trades on Roblox ($12k capital for $760 premium), Robin Hood, Etsy, and SoFi despite attractive premiums
Portfolio allocation: Kenneth deployed $27,892 across 3 positions targeting 4%+ weekly return; previous positions expired Aug 1 rather than week-end Friday

strategy_candidates.change: Implement hard rule to avoid earnings-week covered calls unless target strike is 3+ points OTM and historical IV rank below 50th percentile
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth explicitly rejected high-premium trades on Roblox, Robin Hood, Etsy, SoFi due to post-earnings crash risk despite $700-$4,950 premiums available. For stable dividend payers like HIMS and QuantumCape, he executed without hesitation.

strategy_candidates.change: Establish position-size scaling rule: new symbols max 50% of familiar-symbol position size until 3+ weeks of trade history accumulated
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth bought 200 HIMS shares (familiar) vs. 500 QuantumCape shares (new to him) on same day, explicitly stating he needs familiarity before increasing size: 'QuantumCape is relatively new to me...I like to ease myself in'

strategy_candidates.change: Accept premium targets of 2.7%-3.6% weekly on core holdings even when short-duration expirations (Friday) are available, if strike selection reduces assignment risk to <80%
strategy_candidates.evidence: Enphase 2.7% return was 'not great, but it's okay' and Nphase was selected with $35.50 strike despite $35.49 entry, indicating Kenneth accepts thin margins for consistent weekly allocation to the $1,250/mo target.

title: PART 9: July 28, 2025: $180 PER WEEK On HIMS Stock?
